notes: Public skills are procedural guidance, not architecture authority.
Optional skills remain disabled unless a role or phase explicitly activates them.

skills.name: find-skills
skills.summary: Discover future skill gaps and catalog candidates from the public directory.
trigger_conditions: Use when the planner needs to identify missing skills or future capability gaps.
guardrails: Do not auto-install any discovered skill during autonomous experiments.

skills.name: deep-learning-python
skills.summary: General PyTorch training, optimization, and modeling hygiene.
trigger_conditions: Activate for model authoring, optimizer setup, and training loop implementation.
guardrails: Use as coding guidance only; do not copy baseline architectures.

skills.name: ml-pipeline-orchestrator
skills.summary: Patterns for structured experiment flow and reproducible ML pipelines.
trigger_conditions: Use when translating an idea into a tracked sequence of phases and retries.
guardrails: Do not let orchestration advice override originality requirements.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.summary: Root-cause debugging workflow for model and training failures.
trigger_conditions: Activate on tensor, math, runtime, convergence, or checkpoint failures.
guardrails: Prefer diagnosis over trial-and-error patching.

skills.name: llm-evaluation
skills.summary: Evaluation strategies, automated metrics, and judge-style analysis for LLM systems.
trigger_conditions: Use for comparative analysis, qualitative review, and written judgments.
guardrails: Do not let generic benchmark heuristics erase the need for novelty.

skills.name: weights-and-biases
skills.summary: Optional experiment tracking workflow for teams that choose W&B.
trigger_conditions: Enable only when external experiment tracking is explicitly configured.
guardrails: Off by default.
Must not be required for local-only runs.

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.summary: Meta-skill for authoring and refining internal skills.
trigger_conditions: Use only when creating or revising project-specific skills.
guardrails: Never activate during routine training runs.

skills.name: transformers
skills.summary: Reference-only conventions for tokenizer/model APIs and interoperability.
trigger_conditions: Use only for API conventions or tokenizer compatibility questions.
guardrails: Must not be used to scaffold a baseline-style architecture.
Forbidden for reviewer-led originality decisions.

skills.name: pytorch-lightning
skills.summary: Optional high-level training framework guidance.
trigger_conditions: Use only if the project intentionally adopts Lightning.
guardrails: Disabled by default because the framework uses custom training loops.

skills.name: architecture-originality-gate
skills.summary: Reject generic transformer drift and near-copy architectures before implementation or advancement.
trigger_conditions: Activate before coding, before retries, and before advancing phases.
guardrails: Reject generic transformer drift even if reference skills are available.

skills.name: smoke-test-math-and-shapes
skills.summary: Validate forward math, tensor shapes, gradients, and tiny-batch sanity.
trigger_conditions: Use whenever smoke tests fail or a new architecture is introduced.
guardrails: Require tiny-batch overfit or equivalent sanity evidence before passing smoke.

skills.name: rocm-training-debugger
skills.summary: ROCm-first troubleshooting for runtime, dtype, memory, and kernel issues.
trigger_conditions: Use when device, HIP, compile, memory, or dtype failures occur.
guardrails: Prefer preserving ROCm compatibility over silently switching frameworks.

skills.name: phase-gate-rationale
skills.summary: Turn logs and metrics into continue, pivot, or stop judgments.
trigger_conditions: Use after every phase to produce a written next-action decision.
guardrails: Do not advance without explicit rationale grounded in evidence.

skills.name: baseline-comparator
skills.summary: Compare against internal baselines, prior runs, and public references without copying them.
trigger_conditions: Use when deciding whether an idea is promising relative to known references.
guardrails: Comparisons must not pressure the agent into architectural imitation.

skills.name: experiment-ledger-auditor
skills.summary: Ensure retries, budgets, artifacts, and lineage are persisted before new attempts.
trigger_conditions: Use before retries and before issuing final reports.
guardrails: Block advancement if provenance or artifacts are missing.

skills.name: architecture-research-synthesizer
skills.summary: Distill inspirations into design constraints, opportunities, and anti-copy notes.
trigger_conditions: Use when the planner studies external ideas and needs original synthesis.
guardrails: Convert inspirations into constraints and recombination options, not copy recipes.

skills.name: dataset-curriculum-designer
skills.summary: Design smoke, small, and production data curricula with token budgets and leakage checks.
trigger_conditions: Enable when a new dataset curriculum must be designed or revised.
guardrails: Keep leakage checks explicit across phases.

skills.name: scaling-budget-planner
skills.summary: Estimate parameter, optimizer, checkpoint, and token budgets under single-node ROCm constraints.
trigger_conditions: Use before expensive runs and before retries that change scale.
guardrails: Budgets are soft, but every run must record requested and consumed resources.

skills.name: checkpoint-resume-ops
skills.summary: Standardize save policy, crash recovery, resume flow, and report continuity.
trigger_conditions: Use whenever training can span multiple runs or retries.
guardrails: Do not overwrite lineage when resuming or retrying.

skills.name: eval-suite-builder
skills.summary: Assemble metrics, tasks, regression checks, and qualitative prompts for LM evaluation.
trigger_conditions: Enable when an idea requires a specialized evaluation suite beyond defaults.
guardrails: Default to general-purpose LM evaluation unless the idea explicitly narrows scope.

review_requirements: Document anti-copy notes when public inspirations are used.
Record which missing skills were identified versus actually approved.
Any use of transformers must stay at API-convention level.
Architecture originality review must pass before coding changes proceed.
Produce root-cause notes before changing architecture or runtime settings.
External tracking remains off unless explicitly configured.
Record requested and consumed resources for every attempt.
Every phase judgment must include an explicit next action.
Block advancement when originality or ledger evidence is missing.
